lede: A look at October 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rishabh Pant and Justin Williams, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States Army Special Forces
events.desc: Joint Nigerien-American Special Forces are ambushed by Islamic State militants outside the village of Tongo Tongo.

events.title: Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty
events.desc: The Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty is opened for signature.

events.title: Willie Gillis
events.desc: Norman Rockwell's Willie Gillis character debuts on the cover of The Saturday Evening Post.

events.title: Gutzon Borglum
events.desc: Gutzon Borglum begins sculpting Mount Rushmore.

events.title: Great Syrian Revolt
events.desc: Great Syrian Revolt: Rebels led by Fawzi al-Qawuqji capture Hama from the French Mandate of Syria.

events.title: Finnish torpedo boat S2
events.desc: S2, a Finnish Sokol class torpedo boat, sinks during a fierce storm near the coast of Pori in the Gulf of Bothnia, taking with it the whole crew of 53.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Broodseinde is fought between the British and German armies in Flanders.

events.title: Texas A&M University
events.desc: The Agricultural and Mechanical College of Texas (now known as Texas A&M) opens as the first public college in Texas.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The two-day Second Battle of Corinth ends in a Union victory, with General William Rosecrans protecting the critical rail junction of Corinth, Mississippi from Confederate forces under General Earl Van Dorn.

events.title: Nine Years' War
events.desc: Nine Years' War: Piedmontese troops are defeated by the French.

births.name: Buster Keaton
births.line: American film actor, director, and producer (died 1966)

deaths.text: Võ Nguyên Giáp, Vietnamese general and politician, 3rd Minister of Defence for Vietnam
Yu Kuo-hwa, Chinese politician, 32nd Premier of the Republic of China
Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi, French sculptor, designed the Statue of Liberty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Francis of Assisi Conclusion of Creationtide
Independence Day, celebrates the independence of Lesotho from the United Kingdom in 1966.
